integrations.name: Local Computer
integrations.description: Files, folders, and system access on this Mac

can: Read, write, search, and manage files and folders

integrations.name: Google Workspace

can: Send, read, draft, search, label, and archive emails
Create, read, and update calendar events
limitations: Cannot create Google Docs (use Sheets or send content via email instead)

setupSteps: Click 'Sign in with Google' below and choose your account
You'll be asked to approve access — Delt will be able to read and send email, manage your calendar, edit spreadsheets, and browse Drive files
Once approved, all four services are ready to use
integrations.troubleshooting: Sign-in failing? Make sure pop-ups aren't blocked in your browser.

integrations.name: GitHub
integrations.description: Repos, issues, pull requests, code search

can: Create and manage repos, issues, PRs, branches
Read and write files in repos

setupSteps: If you have GitHub CLI installed, your token is detected automatically — just click Connect
Otherwise: go to github.com → Settings → Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens
Click 'Generate new token (classic)' and name it 'Delt'
Check these scopes: repo (access your repositories), read:org (see your organizations), read:user (read your profile)
Copy the token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token rejected? Make sure you chose Classic token (not fine-grained) and checked all three scopes.

integrations.name: Slack

limitations: Cannot create channels or manage workspace settings

setupSteps: Go to api.slack.com/apps → 'Create New App' → 'From scratch'
Name it 'Delt' and pick your workspace
Go to 'OAuth & Permissions' and add these Bot Token scopes: channels:read (view channels), chat:write (send messages), users:read (see member names)
Click 'Install to Workspace' and copy the Bot User OAuth Token
integrations.troubleshooting: Bot not posting? Invite it to the channel first with /invite @Delt

integrations.name: Notion

limitations: Can only access pages explicitly shared with the integration

setupSteps: Go to notion.so/profile/integrations and click 'New integration'
Name it 'Delt', pick your workspace, and make sure these capabilities are checked: Read content, Update content, Insert content
Click Submit and copy the token
Important: open each Notion page you want Delt to access, click ··· → Connections → Add Delt
integrations.troubleshooting: Can't find your pages? Each page must be shared with the integration individually via ··· → Connections.
integrations.tryIt: Search my Notion for meeting notes

integrations.name: Linear
setupSteps: Go to Linear → Settings → Account → API
Click 'Create key', name it 'Delt'
This gives Delt full access to your issues, projects, and teams
Copy the key right away — it's only shown once
integrations.troubleshooting: Key not working? Make sure you copied the full key — it starts with lin_api_

integrations.name: Stripe

can: Create and manage customers, invoices, and subscriptions

setupSteps: Go to dashboard.stripe.com → Developers → API Keys
Copy your Secret key (starts with sk_test_ or sk_live_)
This gives Delt access to your payments, customers, invoices, and subscriptions
Want limited access? Create a Restricted key and enable only Customers, Charges, Invoices, and Subscriptions
integrations.troubleshooting: Using test mode? Make sure the key starts with sk_test_, not sk_live_

integrations.name: Shopify
setupSteps: Go to your Shopify Admin → Settings → Apps → Develop apps
Create an app named 'Delt'
Under Admin API scopes, enable: Products (read and write) and Orders (read only)
Click Install app and copy the Admin API access token
integrations.troubleshooting: Don't see Develop apps? Your store owner needs to enable custom app development in Settings first.

integrations.name: HubSpot

can: Create and manage contacts, deals, and tickets

setupSteps: Go to HubSpot → Settings → Integrations → Private Apps
Create a private app named 'Delt'
Under Scopes, enable read and write access for Contacts and Deals
Click Create app and copy the access token
integrations.troubleshooting: No Private Apps option? This feature requires a HubSpot Professional or Enterprise plan.

integrations.name: Trello

can: Create and manage boards, lists, and cards

setupSteps: Go to trello.com/power-ups/admin and sign in
Create a Power-Up and click 'Generate a new API Key'
Click the Token link next to the key — this lets Delt read and edit your boards, lists, and cards
Click Allow and copy the token
integrations.troubleshooting: Can't find the Token link? It appears right below your API Key after you generate it.

integrations.name: Figma

can: Read and inspect design files, frames, and components
Read and post comments on designs
limitations: Cannot create or edit designs on the canvas — Figma has no write API. To create visual designs, build them as HTML/CSS and deploy or screenshot them.

setupSteps: Go to figma.com → Settings → Personal access tokens
Click 'Generate new token' and name it 'Delt'
Set the File content scope to Read only — Delt can view your designs but won't modify them
Click Generate and copy the token right away — it's only shown once
integrations.troubleshooting: Token not working? Make sure you set the File content scope — tokens with no scopes can't access anything.

integrations.name: VS Code
integrations.description: Workspace files, diagnostics, terminals, editor control

limitations: Requires VS Code to be running with the MCP extension

setupSteps: In VS Code, open Extensions and search for 'vscode-mcp' — install it
Make sure VS Code is running with the extension active
Click Enable below — no API keys needed, Delt connects directly to your running VS Code
integrations.troubleshooting: Not connecting? Make sure VS Code is running and the vscode-mcp extension shows as active.
integrations.tryIt: Show errors in my current file

integrations.name: Airtable

can: Create, read, update, and delete records
limitations: Cannot create new bases or tables — only add records to existing ones

setupSteps: Go to airtable.com/create/tokens and click 'Create new token'
Name it 'Delt' and add these scopes: Read records and Write records
Choose which bases Delt can access — you can limit it to specific ones
Click Create token and copy it
integrations.troubleshooting: Token rejected? Make sure you added at least one base to the token's access list.
integrations.tryIt: Show records from my first table

integrations.name: Apple Apps

can: Create and manage reminders and notes
limitations: macOS only — requires permission grants on first use

setupSteps: Click Enable below
Your Mac will ask for permission to access Reminders, Calendar, and Contacts — click Allow on each
No sign-in needed — Delt uses your existing Mac account
integrations.troubleshooting: Permission denied? Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security and grant access manually.

integrations.name: Microsoft 365

limitations: Cannot create Teams channels or manage Teams settings

setupSteps: Go to Azure Portal → App registrations → New registration
Name it 'Delt' and set the redirect URI to http://localhost
Under API permissions, add Microsoft Graph access for: email (Mail.Read, Mail.Send), calendar (Calendars.ReadWrite), and files (Files.ReadWrite)
Copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID
Using a personal Microsoft account? Enter 'common' as the Tenant ID
integrations.troubleshooting: Getting unauthorized? Make sure you clicked Grant admin consent after adding API permissions.

integrations.name: Twilio
setupSteps: Go to console.twilio.com — your Account SID and Auth Token are right on the dashboard
Click 'Show' next to Auth Token and copy it
Go to Phone Numbers → Manage and copy a number that has SMS and Voice enabled
These credentials let Delt send texts and make calls from your Twilio account
integrations.troubleshooting: Messages not sending? Trial accounts can only text verified numbers — verify the recipient first.
integrations.tryIt: Send a test text to my phone

integrations.name: SendGrid

limitations: Bulk campaigns require verified sender identity

setupSteps: Go to app.sendgrid.com → Settings → API Keys → Create API Key
Name it 'Delt' and choose 'Restricted Access'
Enable: Mail Send (to send emails) and Template Engine (to use your templates)
Click 'Create & View' and copy the key right away — it's only shown once
integrations.troubleshooting: Emails bouncing? You need to verify your sender identity in SendGrid before sending.
integrations.tryIt: Send a test email to myself

integrations.name: Salesforce

can: Create and manage leads, opportunities, accounts, and contacts

setupSteps: Install the Salesforce CLI first — run: npm install -g @salesforce/cli
Then run: sf org login web — a browser window will open to sign you in
This gives Delt access to your leads, opportunities, accounts, contacts, and reports
Once signed in, click Enable below
integrations.troubleshooting: sf command not found? Close and reopen Terminal after installing the Salesforce CLI.

integrations.name: Zoom
setupSteps: Go to marketplace.zoom.us → Develop → Build App → Server-to-Server OAuth
Name it 'Delt' and create the app
Add scopes: meeting:read (view meetings) and meeting:write (create and update meetings)
Activate the app, then copy the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret
integrations.troubleshooting: App stuck on inactive? Make sure you added both scopes and clicked Activate.
integrations.tryIt: Schedule a meeting for tomorrow at 2pm

integrations.name: Custom API
integrations.description: Connect any service that has an API key

can: Fetch data from any HTTP API
Send requests with custom headers and auth
limitations: Depends on the target API's capabilities

setupSteps: Find the API docs for the service you want to connect
Create a new API key in their settings
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Not working? Double-check the API key and make sure it has the right permissions.
